# UC-22.2.43 · NIS2 Annual Security Assessment Completion Tracking

## Description

Compares each NIS2-scoped legal entity’s next due date from the assessment register with the latest closed ServiceNow GRC ticket and optional penetration-test report timestamps so overdue or unsigned annual work surfaces before the board pack is written.

## Value

CFO, legal, and the CISO office receive a single pane showing which entities risk missing independent assurance cycles, which makes budget and third-party sourcing decisions defensible before external audit or supervisory dialogue.

## Implementation

(1) Populate `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv` from the authoritative entity list. (2) Standardise ticket titles with “NIS2 annual assessment”. (3) Ingest pentest summaries into `index=pentest`. (4) Schedule weekly; notify entity owners when `overdue=1`. (5) Archive dashboards to `index=audit_evidence` after sign-off.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• ServiceNow instance reachable for ticket validation; Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) configured with OAuth or basic auth per your security standard, `inputs.conf` intervals typically 300 seconds for incident/task tables.
• Lookup `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv` in `apps/search/lookups/` with RBAC limiting edit rights to Compliance Admins.
• Indexes `itsm`, `pentest`, `vuln` created on indexers; roles granted via `authorize.conf`.
• Field aliases: map ServiceNow `u_company` to `company` if required.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable incident/task or custom GRC table pulls in the Splunkbase 1928 app (`local/inputs.conf`). Ensure sourcetype `snow:sn_grc_policy` or `snow:task` carries `closed_at`, `state`, `short_description`, `company`. For consultants’ PDF reports, use HEC (`inputs.conf` token on heavy forwarder) posting JSON with `sourcetype=pentest:report`, fields `entity`, `closed_date`, `report_id`. Optional: pull Qualys/Tenable extracts into `index=vuln` for corroboration — keep `sourcetype` explicit in props.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Schedule weekly Monday 08:00 with `earliest=-400d` on subsearches to capture annual cycles.

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_assessment_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| join legal_entity_id type=left [
    search index=itsm (sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy OR sourcetype=snow:task)
      short_description="*NIS2*annual*assessment*" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(closed_at) as last_closed latest(state) as ticket_state by company
    | rename company as legal_entity_id
]
| join legal_entity_id type=left [
    search index=pentest sourcetype=pentest:report earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(closed_date) as last_pen_report by entity
    | rename entity as legal_entity_id
]
| where overdue=1
```

Add `lookup nis2_assessment_kfp_exceptions` for suppression. Alert when `isnull(last_closed)` OR `strptime(last_closed,"%Y-%m-%d")<due_epoch`.

Step 3 — Validate
Select an entity known to be green: confirm both ServiceNow `closed_at` and register `next_assessment_due` yield `overdue=0`. For a synthetic overdue row, leave `next_assessment_due` in the past and ensure the alert fires. Reconcile counts with a ServiceNow list view filtered on assignment group.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard tiles: count overdue, table split by `assessment_type`, link drilldown to entity CMDB. Alert email template should cite clause Art.21(2)(f) context for executives. Store quarterly PDF snapshots via `outputlookup` audit staging.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Join drops all rows** — verify `company` matches `legal_entity_id` case-sensitivity; use `| eval legal_entity_id=lower(legal_entity_id)` consistently.
• **Mass false overdue after CSV import** — date format mismatch; enforce `%Y-%m-%d` in `strptime`.
• **Empty pentest index** — HEC token wrong index; check `Metrics` in Cloud Monitoring for 4xx.
• **RBAC errors** — user role missing `pentest` index; update `Settings` > `Roles`.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_assessment_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| join legal_entity_id type=left [
    search index=itsm (sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy OR sourcetype=snow:task)
      short_description="*NIS2*annual*assessment*" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(closed_at) as last_closed latest(state) as ticket_state by company
    | rename company as legal_entity_id
]
| join legal_entity_id type=left [
    search index=pentest sourcetype=pentest:report earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(closed_date) as last_pen_report by entity
    | rename entity as legal_entity_id
]
| where overdue=1
| lookup nis2_assessment_kfp_exceptions legal_entity_id OUTPUT exception_until
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table legal_entity_id, assessment_type, next_assessment_due, last_closed, last_pen_report, overdue
```

## Visualization

Table of overdue entities, timeline of `last_closed`, bar chart of `assessment_type`.
